era_context: Continuation of the Iron Age II Eastern Mediterranean and Mesopotamia. The Neo-Assyrian Empire was in the reign of Shalmaneser IV. The Levantine kingdoms (Israel, Judah, Phoenician city-states, Aramean polities) were navigating the growing Assyrian threat. Greece was in the Archaic period; the Olympic Games had been established (traditionally dated 776 BCE, though this is debated). Egypt remained fragmented.
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are reliably attested to 788 BCE in surviving sources. The year falls within Shalmaneser IV's reign, but no contemporary inscriptions or chronicles document particular campaigns, political events, or cultural developments for this specific year.
historiographic_note: This year is essentially undocumented in the historical record. Assyrian king lists provide reign lengths but not year-by-year detail. The absence of evidence reflects both the limitations of ancient record-keeping and the loss of most cuneiform tablets and inscriptions. For most ancient years outside major political or military events, specific year-by-year documentation does not exist.
